title: pasta: system tests fail under IPv6
body:
Bring up 1minutetip VM (Fedora 39).
```console
# dnf install podman-tests
# loginctl enable-linger fedora
# su - fedora
$ bats /usr/share/podman/test/system/505-networking-pasta.bats
[ tons and tons and tons of errors, common factor is IPv6 ]
```
e.g.
```
   [13:17:16.243623304] $ podman run --net=pasta:-6 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 ip -j -4 address show
   [13:17:16.376916936] Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
   No external routable interface for IPv6
   External interface not usable
   [13:17:16.379969866] [ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
```
   [13:17:16.708433156] $ podman run --net=pasta quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 ip -j -6 address show
   [13:17:17.017262587] [{"ifindex":1,"ifname":"lo","flags":["LOOPBACK","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65536,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":
"inet6","local":"::1","prefixlen":128,"scope":"host","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]},{"ifindex":2,"ifname":"eth0","flags":["BRO
ADCAST","MULTICAST","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65520,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet6","local":"fe80::f46e:9eff:fe44:4b55","prefixl
en":64,"scope":"link","tentative":true,"valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]}]
   Device "null" does not exist.
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: Container address not matching host
   #| expected: = ''
   #|   actual:   null
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
```
   [13:17:17.431924250] $ podman run --net=pasta:-a,2001:db8::1 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 ip -j -6 address show
   [13:17:17.746388751] [{"ifindex":1,"ifname":"lo","flags":["LOOPBACK","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65536,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":
"inet6","local":"::1","prefixlen":128,"scope":"host","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]},{"ifindex":2,"ifname":"eth0","flags":["BRO
ADCAST","MULTICAST","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65520,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet6","local":"fe80::5cd2:c4ff:fe1f:77bc","prefixl
en":64,"scope":"link","tentative":true,"valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]}]
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: Container address not matching configured value
   #| expected: = 2001:db8::1
   #|   actual:   null
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
```
   [13:17:45.819629632] $ podman run --net=pasta -p []:5207:5207/tcp quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 sh -c for port in $(seq 5207 5207); do                   
           socat -u TCP6-LISTEN:${port} STDOUT &                          done; wait
   [13:17:45.889865930] Error: must provide a non-empty container host IP to publish
   [13:17:45.893574737] [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
For posterity, this is how to disable ipv6 in Fedora 39:
```console
# nmcli connection modify eth0 ipv6.method "disabled"
# nmcli connection up eth0
Connection successfully activated (D-Bus active path: /org/freedesktop/NetworkManager/ActiveConnection/5)
```

comments.body:
Tests fail for you only if IPv6 is disabled, right?

That is, I'm trying to understand if the issue is rather in the test logic that decides whether to run the IPv6 tests or not. Because if IPv6 connectivity is disabled, it doesn't make a lot of sense to try:

```
$ podman run --net=pasta:-6 quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 ip -j -4 address show
```

which means: run pasta with IPv4 disabled, check that no IPv4 address exists. The `has_ipv6` check (from `skip_if_no_ipv6`) simply does:

```
[ -n "$(ip -j -6 route show | jq -rM '.[] | select(.dst == "default")')" ]
```

pasta doesn't consider your IPv6 connectivity good enough to start (with `-6 / --ipv6-only`) on your system, but this check passes. `ip -6 route show` for this situation will probably point to the right direction.

>if the issue is rather in the test logic that decides whether to run the IPv6 tests or not

That is my assumption. I don't know enough about IPv6 to know how to debug. This is what I know:
* tests fail on regular fresh f39 install
* my solution is `nmcli connection modify eth0 ipv6.method "disabled" ; nmcli connection up eth0`. This makes tests pass.

```console
# ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host noprefixroute 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: eth0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc fq_codel state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether fa:16:3e:50:7a:9c brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    altname enp0s3
    altname ens3
    inet 10.0.187.90/22 brd 10.0.187.255 scope global dynamic noprefixroute eth0
       valid_lft 42760sec preferred_lft 42760sec
    inet6 2620:52:0:bb:f816:3eff:fe50:7a9c/64 scope global dynamic noprefixroute 
       valid_lft 2591883sec preferred_lft 604683sec
    inet6 fe80::f816:3eff:fe50:7a9c/64 scope link noprefixroute 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever

# ip -6 route show
2620:52:0:bb::/64 dev eth0 proto ra metric 100 pref medium
fe80::/64 dev eth0 proto kernel metric 1024 pref medium
default proto static metric 100 pref medium
        nexthop via 2620:52:0:bb::fe dev eth0 weight 1 
        nexthop via fe80::f816:3eff:fe33:6758 dev eth0 weight 1 
        nexthop via fe80::4e16:fc01:558c:98ed dev eth0 weight 1 
        nexthop via fe80::4e16:fc01:5583:88aa dev eth0 weight 1 
```

The issue is caused by the fact that we only have a multipath default route for IPv6, and pasta(1) is unable to digest the related netlink attribute (there's no RTA_OIF outside the RTA_MULTIPATH attribute, which we currently ignore). So, if IPv4 is disabled via `-6` (which is the case for a number of IPv6 tests), pasta doesn't find any interface which can be used to route traffic, and refuses to start.

Now pending review: https://archives.passt.top/passt-dev/20240201233257.2287011-1-sbrivio@redhat.com/

This should now be fixed in Fedora package versions `passt-0^20240220.g1e6f92b-1.fc39`, `passt-0^20240220.g1e6f92b-1.fc40`, `passt-0^20240220.g1e6f92b-1.fc41`, and Debian's `0.0~git20240216.08344da-1`.

Not fixed in `0^20240220.g1e6f92b-1.fc39`:
```
✗ [505] IPv6 default address assignment                                                                                                                        
   (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 235,                                                                                        
    from function `assert' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 929,                                                                                          
    in test file test/system/505-networking-pasta.bats, line 302)                                                                                               
     `assert "${container_address}" = "${host_address}" \' failed                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                
   [10:53:04.139425092] $ /root/go/podman/bin/podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                
   [10:53:04.202222568] $ /root/go/podman/bin/podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}                                                            
                                                                                                                                                                
   [10:53:04.275709384] $ /root/go/podman/bin/podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}                                                     
   [10:53:04.338887942] quay.io/libpod/testimage:20240123 1f6acd4c4a1d                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                
   [10:53:04.496754779] $ /root/go/podman/bin/podman run --net=pasta quay.io/libpod/testimage:20240123 ip -j -6 address show                                    
   [10:53:04.777530485] [{"ifindex":1,"ifname":"lo","flags":["LOOPBACK","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65536,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":
"inet6","local":"::1","prefixlen":128,"scope":"host","protocol":"kernel_lo","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295}]},{"ifindex":2,"ifnam
e":"eth0","flags":["BROADCAST","MULTICAST","UP","LOWER_UP"],"mtu":65520,"operstate":"UNKNOWN","txqlen":1000,"addr_info":[{"family":"inet6","local":"2620:52:0:bb
:f816:3eff:fe7e:b17e","prefixlen":64,"scope":"global","tentative":true,"noprefixroute":true,"valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_life_time":4294967295},{"fam
ily":"inet6","local":"fe80::1059:3fff:fe8e:df74","prefixlen":64,"scope":"link","tentative":true,"protocol":"kernel_ll","valid_life_time":4294967295,"preferred_l
ife_time":4294967295}]}]                                                                                                                                        
   Device "null" does not exist.                                                                                                                                
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv                                                                                                              
   #|     FAIL: Container address not matching host                                                                                                             
   #| expected: = ''                                                                                                                                            
   #|   actual:   2620:52:0:bb:f816:3eff:fe7e:b17e                                                                                                              
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^              
```
Solution:
```console
# nmcli connection show; nmcli connection modify eth0 ipv6.method "disabled" ; nmcli connection up eth0
```

> Not fixed in `0^20240220.g1e6f92b-1.fc39`:

No, no, wait:

> ```
>    #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv                                                                                                              
>    #|     FAIL: Container address not matching host                                                                                                             
>    #| expected: = ''                                                                                                                                            
>    #|   actual:   2620:52:0:bb:f816:3eff:fe7e:b17e                                                                                                              
>    #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^              
> ```

...the issue itself is actually fixed: IPv6 is now properly configured in the container, with an address sourced from the host (2620:52:0:bb:f816:3eff:fe7e:b17e).

But the test script doesn't get the address from the host (`expected: = ''`) as it doesn't select any interface, because it doesn't know how to read multipath routes. I have to fix the test.

This should now be fixed by https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22058 -- note that I can't test it using your same steps, as all my environments (and thus also the 1minutetip Fedora VM) get a single IPv6 address with a single default gateway via SLAAC (and optionally DHCPv6).
